title: Supplement ABCG2 Antagonism — Pharmacological Risk Assessment

target.name: ABCG2 (Breast Cancer Resistance Protein / BCRP)
target.function: ATP-driven urate efflux transporter; apical surface of enterocytes exports uric acid from blood into gut lumen. Accounts for ~33% of total gut urate secretion. Loss-of-function Q141K variant (rs2231142) reduces surface expression ~50% and is enriched in East Asian populations.

gut_model_parameters.description: Reference parameters for the small intestine gut-lumen compartment relevant to ABCG2-mediated urate transport
gut_model_parameters.volume_source: Commonly cited reference value for fasted small intestinal fluid volume; Johnson 1997 GI physiology; 250 mL is the conservative (high) estimate — lower volume estimates (100-150 mL proximal SI) would increase effective concentration
gut_model_parameters.ph_source: Proximal small intestine pH range; ABCG2 is expressed throughout SI with peak in duodenum/jejunum
gut_model_parameters.transit_note: Small intestine transit ~2-4h; solubility-limited drugs reach peak concentration in proximal SI before dilution/transit
gut_model_parameters.abcg2_expression_note: ABCG2 highest in duodenum and proximal jejunum (apical surface). The 250 mL whole-SI estimate over-dilutes relative to the proximal compartment where ABCG2 density is highest — effective inhibition concentrations may be higher than model outputs suggest.
gut_model_parameters.modeling_approach: Effective dissolved concentration = min(dose-limited concentration, solubility cap). Risk ratio = effective concentration / IC50. Hill equation with n=1: fractional inhibition = ratio / (ratio + 1).

risk_thresholds.NOT_APPLICABLE: Expression-regulation mechanism; IC50 occupancy framework not applicable

results.data_note: Hoechst 33342 is a fluorescent ABCG2 substrate; assay measures inhibition of its efflux. MCF7 cells overexpress ABCG2 vs enterocytes — functional inhibition in primary enterocytes may differ.

results.source: PubMed search 2026-05-05; Yu 2024 in vivo gout study showed net-favorable urate direction (likely indirect anti-inflammatory effects dominating over ABCG2 expression changes)
results.data_note: The §1.14 Caco-2 transwell assay (48h, acute urate flux) will not detect EGCG's mechanism. A separate longer-treatment arm (72h) with ABCG2 Western blot is needed to quantify EGCG's expression effect.
results.mechanism_note: EGCG downregulates ABCG2 mRNA and protein expression over 24-72h in cell culture. Effect is transcriptional/post-transcriptional, not acute transport inhibition. No kinetic IC50 for direct transport block exists in ChEMBL.

results.data_note: Mitoxantrone is a high-affinity ABCG2 substrate; assay measures inhibition of its efflux. MCF7-VP cells overexpress ABCG2. Single value — no replication across labs in ChEMBL for this specific assay.
